title: Europe Just Shattered 150 Years of Heat Records
script: Europe just shattered June records! The UK hit 37.3 degrees Celsius this week, smashing its hottest June temperature ever recorded and breaking a mark that stood since 1976. Denmark hit 37.2 degrees, its hottest day since 1874. Basel in Switzerland climbed to 38 degrees. These aren't just records, they're obliterating temperatures that lasted through entire generations. The UK Health Security Agency confirmed four heat deaths in two days, including a 22-year-old and a 15-year-old. The heat is now pushing east toward Czech Republic and Austria. Meteorologists say June temperatures like this were essentially impossible under historical climate patterns. So is 37 degrees just June now?

shots.phrase: Denmark hit 37.2 degrees, its hottest since 1874
Basel in Switzerland climbed to 38 degrees
heat is pushing east toward Czech Republic
